answer.text: Minority investors to block any sale transaction
Majority shareholders to force minority shareholders to sell on the same terms
The custodian to sell fund assets
Investors to redeem units at any time

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Drag along rights allow majority shareholders (often the promoter group) to force minority shareholders (including AIF investors) to sell their stakes when a buyer wants to acquire 100% of the company. The minority must sell on the same terms as the majority. **The Trap:** While drag along forces minorities to sell, it ensures they get the same price and terms as majority — it's not necessarily disadvantageous. It facilitates clean exits for the entire company. **Cross-Exam Context:** Drag along is tested alongside tag along in XIX-A and XIX-C. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Drag along can actually benefit AIF investors by facilitating full company exits. Without drag along, a single holdout investor could block an otherwise favorable acquisition.
